description: Godot Editor plugin for creating and editing FurnitureData resources with visual sprite selection, property form, collision override, and live preview

userStories.title: Add collision override properties to FurnitureData
userStories.description: As a developer, I need collision_size_override and collision_offset properties on FurnitureData so the creator tool can store custom collision areas.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '@export var collision_size_override: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO' to scripts/furniture_data.gd — when non-zero, used instead of auto-calculated size
Add '@export var collision_offset: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO' to scripts/furniture_data.gd — offset from sprite center
Update furniture.gd _ready(): if data.collision_size_override != Vector2.ZERO, use it as shape.size instead of tex_size; apply collision_offset to collision_shape.position
Existing .tres files remain compatible — new properties default to zero so current auto-sizing behavior is unchanged
Verify all 5 existing items (table, sofa, toy, food_bowl, bookshelf) still work correctly with no behavior change
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold editor plugin with plugin.cfg and main dock
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Furniture Creator to register as a Godot 4 EditorPlugin so I can activate it from Project Settings > Plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Create addons/furniture_creator/plugin.cfg with correct Godot 4 format (name, description, author, version, script)
Create addons/furniture_creator/plugin.gd extending EditorPlugin
On _enter_tree(), add a custom dock/panel to the editor (use add_control_to_bottom_panel or add_control_to_dock)
The dock contains a placeholder Label 'Furniture Creator' to confirm it loads
On _exit_tree(), remove the dock cleanly
Plugin can be enabled in Project Settings > Plugins without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build property form with all FurnitureData fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a form with all FurnitureData properties so I can configure items visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace placeholder label with a ScrollContainer containing the property form

Form fields grouped: Identity (id, display_name) > Economics (coin_cost, discard_refund_ratio) > Behavior (walkable, walk_surface_y_offset, can_fall_off_edge, jumpable, stackable, interaction_type, interaction_coin_bonus, interaction_cooldown) > Visual (display_scale)
id field auto-generates from display_name as snake_case but is editable
interaction_type is a dropdown with options: (none), play, eat, sleep
interaction_coin_bonus and interaction_cooldown only visible when interaction_type is not (none)
walk_surface_y_offset only visible when walkable is checked
discard_refund_ratio is a SpinBox (0.0–1.0 step 0.05) with a label showing calculated refund: 'Refund: X coins'
display_scale defaults to Vector2(4, 4)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add atlas sprite sheet region picker
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to browse a sprite sheet and click-drag to select a region so I can pick sprites visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'From Atlas' tab/section at the top of the form
A file picker button defaults to res://assets/Top-Down_Retro_Interior/ and filters for .png files
Selecting a file displays the full sprite sheet in a scrollable, zoomable TextureRect area
Click-and-drag on the sheet defines a selection rectangle shown as a colored overlay
The selected region (x, y, w, h) is displayed in 4 SpinBox fields for manual fine-tuning
Editing the SpinBox values updates the visual selection rectangle
The resulting AtlasTexture region is stored for use when saving
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add standalone image import option
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to import a standalone image file so I can use custom sprites not on an atlas.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'From File' tab/section alongside 'From Atlas'
A file picker allows selecting any image file from the project (filters: *.png, *.jpg, *.svg, *.tres)
Selecting a file loads it as a Texture2D and displays it in the preview
Switching between 'From Atlas' and 'From File' tabs clears the other's selection
The texture reference is stored for use when saving
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add live sprite preview at display scale
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the selected sprite at its configured display_scale so I can verify it looks correct.
acceptanceCriteria: A preview panel (right side or bottom of the dock) shows the selected sprite
Sprite is rendered at the current display_scale value (e.g., 4x4 by default)
Preview updates in real-time when sprite selection changes or display_scale changes
If the scaled sprite is larger than the preview area, the preview is scrollable
When no sprite is selected, show a placeholder message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add collision area override controls with preview overlay
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to toggle custom collision and see the collision rectangle overlaid on the preview so I can tune it for items like the sofa.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Custom collision' checkbox in the Visual section of the form (below display_scale)
When unchecked, collision is auto-calculated (texture_size * display_scale) — shown as a label
When checked, width/height SpinBoxes appear pre-filled with auto-calculated values, plus an offset Vector2 field
The collision rectangle is drawn as a semi-transparent colored overlay on the sprite preview
Overlay updates in real-time as collision size/offset values change
Auto-calculated collision (checkbox unchecked) also shows the overlay so the developer can see the default area
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Save FurnitureData as .tres resource file
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to click Save and have the tool generate a valid .tres file in res://furniture/data/ so the item is immediately available in the shop.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Save' button at the bottom of the form
Validates required fields before saving: id, display_name, and texture must be set — shows error message if missing
Creates a FurnitureData resource with all configured properties
For atlas sprites: creates AtlasTexture sub-resource with the selected region
For standalone images: references the Texture2D directly
Saves to res://furniture/data/{id}.tres using ResourceSaver.save()
If file already exists, shows a confirmation dialog before overwriting
After saving, the file is visible in Godot's FileSystem dock
The saved .tres file loads correctly in the existing shop system (shop.gd scans res://furniture/data/)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load existing .tres files for editing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to load an existing FurnitureData .tres file into the tool so I can tweak properties and re-save.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Load' button at the top of the form
Opens a file dialog filtered to res://furniture/data/*.tres
Loading populates all form fields with the item's current property values
If the item uses an AtlasTexture, switches to 'From Atlas' mode and restores the atlas path and region selection
If the item uses a standalone Texture2D, switches to 'From File' mode and shows the texture
Collision override fields are populated if collision_size_override != Vector2.ZERO
Saving after load overwrites the original file (with confirmation)
A 'New' button resets all fields to defaults for creating a fresh item
Typecheck passes
